patch.value: Opt-in native comment sync (GitHub comments → pm comments collection)

Add --comments-mode body|annotations|both (default body, byte-identical to today). annotations/both sync GitHub issue comments into the pm item's native comments collection via the SDK comments() primitive, deduping on re-sync by a stable GitHub-comment-id marker.

value.author: pi-agent
value.text: Verified: default mode byte-identical (composeBody pinned in tests + real import of unbraind/pm-cli#574); annotations mode populated native comments for 16 real issues; re-import deduped (42 comments unchanged). 12 new tests + 99 existing all green. peerDependency floor unchanged (comments() SDK fn pre-existed at 2026.7.10).

patch.value: Implemented and verified; ready for PR review

value.author: pi-agent
value.text: Greptile review (2 P2 findings, both fixed): (1) commentsMode validation now validates-before-assign instead of assign-then-detect; (2) --with-comments + --comments-mode annotations now reconciles to 'both' rather than silently dropping the legacy flag (documented in README + test). Re-ran release:check: 112 tests pass, 0 vulns, changelog up to date.

value.author: claude-fleet
value.text: Correction to the 21:41 verification note (flagged by CodeRabbit on PR #33): (1) the peerDependency floor WAS bumped to >=2026.7.14 in commit 426672e — the comments() SDK primitive is public only since pm CLI 2026.7.14, and the static ESM import would have killed extension load on older hosts, so it is now lazy-loaded and the floor states the real requirement (package.json + package-lock both updated in 54a46a4). (2) Test math: 13 new tests in test/comments-sync.test.ts + 99 existing = 112 total, matching the release:check total; the earlier '12 new' was a miscount.
